_about: Endpoint field-quirk map for sprite IMAGE generation through `vg generate run <endpoint_id> --<field> <value>`. Different endpoints name the same concept differently (image_url vs image_urls). This map saves the agent from re-deriving each schema. It is NOT a guarantee of availability or current defaults: confirm with `vg generate schema <endpoint_id> --json` before relying on a field, and `vg generate models <query> --json` to discover endpoints. Endpoint IDs are passed to the CLI verbatim — never rewrite them.

_usage.image: vg generate run <endpoint_id> --prompt "..." [--image_urls '["<url>"]'] --aspect_ratio 4:3 --resolution 1K --download out.png --json   (edit endpoints take --image_urls, a JSON array; t2i endpoints omit it)
_usage.queue: Most generation endpoints are queue-based — fire with --async, poll with `vg generate status`. Background removal (bria) is synchronous: run it without --async.

nano-banana-pro-t2i.note: Recommended default for character/anchor generation: fast, strong on chunky pixel art.

nano-banana-pro-edit.note: Edit endpoint: derive directional anchors and labeled pose boards from an approved character image (the pose-board step uses this endpoint). For a pose board pass --aspect_ratio 4:3.

gpt-image-2-t2i.note: Stronger character consistency + text rendering; slower and pricier than nano-banana-pro.

bria-background-remove.note: Semantic segmentation (not chroma key) — works even on white-on-white. Synchronous: do not pass --async. Alternative to chroma_clean.py when you did not generate on a flat matte.
